title: Houses for Rent in Detroit-Shoreway, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Detroit-Shoreway, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in Detroit-Shoreway (44102), Cleveland. Rent ranges, RTA access, and Section 8-friendly homes from a local property manager. Book a showing.
answerBox: Detroit-Shoreway is a near-west-side Cleveland neighborhood in ZIP 44102, home to the Gordon Square Arts District and the Detroit Avenue business corridor. We manage Section 8-friendly rental homes in and around Detroit-Shoreway; homes in our portfolio there typically rent from about $700 to $1,800 a month, with most closer to $1,000.

body: Detroit-Shoreway is a near-west-side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44102**, running roughly along the **Detroit Avenue** corridor between Cudell and Ohio City, about three miles west of Downtown. The neighborhood contains the **Gordon Square Arts District** and sits a short drive from the West Shoreway (Route 2) along Lake Erie.

Housing stock is a mix of early-1900s two-family duplexes and single-family houses on blocks around W. 58th, W. 65th, and W. 76th Streets, with front porches and small yards typical of Cleveland's near-west side. We manage Section 8-friendly rental homes in and around Detroit-Shoreway as part of our broader West Side inventory — [book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house in Detroit-Shoreway?
body: Citywide, a 2-bedroom home runs a median of about $1,100 a month and a 3-bedroom about $1,350, per Zumper's rent report dated July 2026 — a reasonable starting point for Detroit-Shoreway. Homes in our own portfolio in this part of Cleveland typically run from about $700 to $1,800 a month depending on size and condition, with most renting closer to $1,000.

RentCafe's report dated July 2026 on professionally managed Cleveland apartments shows a citywide average of $1,564 a month; that figure leans toward newer managed buildings rather than the individually owned houses and duplexes more typical of Detroit-Shoreway's residential streets.

Detroit-Shoreway is served by GCRTA bus routes running along Detroit Avenue connecting to Downtown and Lakewood, and sits close to Red Line rail service a short drive or bus ride south near Ohio City. The West Shoreway (Route 2) gives quick highway access west toward Lakewood and Westlake or east into Downtown.

items: **Transit:** Detroit Avenue bus routes to Downtown; Red Line access nearby via Ohio City
**Nearby:** Gordon Square Arts District, Battery Park green space
**Highway access:** West Shoreway (Route 2) to Downtown and western suburbs

body: Detroit-Shoreway (44102) is one of the ZIP codes where we currently manage rental homes, and every one of them accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have its own source-of-income protection ordinance — but our policy welcomes vouchers on every home we manage, no exceptions.

If you have a CMHA voucher and want to see what's available in or near Detroit-Shoreway, [book a free showing](/p/book-showing), or read our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for details on applying, waitlists, and inspections. CMHA's own [Housing Choice Voucher application page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) has current program details.

Detroit-Shoreway borders the [Gordon Square Arts District](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-gordon-square-cleveland/) to its east along Detroit Avenue and the [Edgewater lakefront](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-edgewater-cleveland/) to its north, so renters comparing nearby West Side options may want to review both.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Detroit-Shoreway?
faq.a: Yes. Detroit-Shoreway (44102) is one of the neighborhoods where we currently manage rental homes, and every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to see what's available now.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Detroit-Shoreway?
faq.a: Citywide, comparable 2-bedroom homes run about $1,100 a month and 3-bedrooms about $1,350, per Zumper's July 2026 data. Homes in our own Detroit-Shoreway-area portfolio typically rent from about $700 to $1,800, with most closer to $1,000.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Detroit-Shoreway in?
faq.a: Detroit-Shoreway is in ZIP code 44102 on Cleveland's near-west side, running along the Detroit Avenue corridor between Cudell and Ohio City.

faq.q: Is Detroit-Shoreway close to Downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Yes, Detroit-Shoreway is about three miles west of Downtown, with bus routes along Detroit Avenue and highway access via the West Shoreway (Route 2).

faq.q: What is the Gordon Square Arts District?
faq.a: Gordon Square is an arts and entertainment district within Detroit-Shoreway, along Detroit Avenue, with several theaters and restaurants. It's a walkable subsection of the broader Detroit-Shoreway neighborhood, also in ZIP 44102.
